calendar.matters:
Motion For Reconsideration Of Order Granting Writ Of Administrative Mandate
Off Calendar May-16-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. CAROLYNE EVANS, ATTORNEY GENERAL, TEL. NO. 1-415-722-6040 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Petitioner James O'Dorisio's motion for reconsideration of the order granting writ of administrative mandate is denied. Dr. O'Dorisio has not provided any new or different facts, circumstances, or law which he could not, with reasonable diligence, have presented at the time of the prior motion. Nonetheless, the court reviewed the prior order and determined that it should stand undisturbed. Nothing in Richardson v. Lewis (1988) 203 Cal.App.3d 486 warrants a modification of the April 14, 2017 order. Nor do Dr. O'Dorisio's concerns about the fairness of his hearing before the Medical Board of California warrant modifying the order. There is an insufficient showing to make a finding or to presume that the Board will not provide Dr. O'Dorisio a full and fair hearing on remand. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Not reported. =302/hk

MOTION / Notice Of Hearing And Motion For Peremptory Writ Of Mandamus [Ccp 1094.5]
Off Calendar Apr-14-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. MICHAEL A. FIRESTONE, TEL. NO. 1-650-212-4900 APPEARING FOR THE PETITIOENR;
ATTY. CAROLYNE EVANS & ATTY. JANE ZACK SIMON, DEPUTY ATTORNEY GENERAL, TEL. NO. 1-415-703-1211 APPEARING FOR THE RESPONDEN

RULING - A R G U ED ; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Petitioner James O'Dorisio's petition for writ of administrative mandate is granted. The case is remanded to Respondent Medical Board of California for reassessment of the penalty against Dr. O'Dorisio. The increased practice monitor probationary term imposed by the Board after this court reversed four of the Board's findings and remanded for assessment of discipline and the Board itself reversed two additional findings is grossly excessive and a manifest abuse of discretion. While perhaps not intended to be punitive, the increased penalty appears punitive and in retaliation for Dr. O'Dorisio exercising his right to file the prior writ because: 1) the probationary terms imposed in December 2016 were more severe than those imposed in August 2015, even though in December 2016 there had been a substantial reduction in the number of findings of improper treatment; and 2) in August 2015, at the time of the Board's initial decision, the Board did not perceive that the likelihood of harm to the public required the presence of a practice monitor while Dr. O'Dorisio performed carotid endarterectomy procedures and nothing has occurred since August 2015 to show that the likelihood of harm to the public has increased. The practice monitor's reports and the safeguards implemented by Dr. O'Dorisio strongly suggest that the likelihood of harm to the public has decreased, not increased. Because the authority to fix a penalty is primarily vested in the Board, Dr. O'Dorisio's request that this court order the Board to reduce the probation period or strike the practice monitor penalty entirely is denied. (Richardson v. Board of Supervisors (1988) 203 Cal.App.3d 486, 495.) However, this court can and does direct the Board to fix a penalty at a level no greater than the original penalty set in August 2015. (Id.) Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Laura A. Reding, CSR 711 =302/HK

Stay Of Administrative Decision And Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandate
Off Calendar Jan-09-2017 Granted Per Written Order
